name: Poke by the Pound
tagline: Industrial-park address, zero ambiance, insane poke — Kalihi's best-kept secret that locals don't want you to know about.
mustOrder: Shoyu ahi — classic and perfectly seasoned, sold by the pound
Spicy salmon — creamy spicy sauce with fresh salmon
Hawaiian-style ahi — traditional preparation with limu and Hawaiian salt
parking: Industrial park lot with plenty of free stalls
talkStory: Run by the Sagawinit family out of a Kalihi industrial park since the mid-2010s — originally started selling poke to warehouse workers and construction crews in the neighborhood. The name is literal: buy by the pound instead of by the bowl and save 30-40% compared to Waikiki poke shops. Fresh ahi delivered daily from local fishing boats. The industrial-park location with zero ambiance is a feature, not a bug — it keeps tourist traffic near zero and prices honest. Grab a pound, buy rice separately, and feed two people.
